ragas.name: Nattai
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; associated with grandeur and heroism; 'Nata' and 'Nattai' are used interchangeably in the tradition

ragas.name: Gowla
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; evokes pathos and devotion

ragas.name: Arabhi
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; majestic and expansive character

ragas.name: Varali
ragas.notes: Janya of Tanarupi (6th melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; considered auspicious yet difficult; associated with Ganesha

ragas.name: Sri
ragas.notes: Janya of Kharaharapriya (22nd melakarta); one of the five Ghana ragas; associated with grandeur and devotion; considered the crown raga of the Pancharatna set

ragas.name: Todi
ragas.notes: 8th melakarta; one of the most expansive ragas in the Carnatic canon; evokes pathos, longing, and deep introspection; a favourite for extended alapana

ragas.name: Bhairavi
ragas.notes: Janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); distinct from Sindhu Bhairavi; evokes pathos and devotion; traditionally used to close concerts

ragas.name: Sindhu Bhairavi
ragas.notes: Distinct from Bhairavi; janya of Natabhairavi (20th melakarta); popularised in the 20th century; lighter emotional register than Bhairavi; widely used in film music

ragas.name: Kalyani
ragas.notes: 65th melakarta (Mechakalyani); all sharp notes except natural Sa and Pa; associated with brightness, auspiciousness, and devotion

ragas.name: Shankarabharanam
ragas.notes: 29th melakarta; equivalent to the Western major scale; considered the most complete and balanced raga; parent of Nata and Arabhi

ragas.name: Kambhoji
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); one of the most popular ragas; associated with bhakti and grandeur; a staple of the concert repertoire

ragas.name: Begada
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); associated with devotion and serenity; a favourite of the Tyagaraja school

ragas.name: Saveri
ragas.notes: Janya of Dheerashankarabharanam (29th melakarta); evokes pathos and longing; associated with morning hours

ragas.name: Kedaram
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); associated with devotion and grandeur; a favourite for kritis in the Tyagaraja corpus

ragas.name: Natabhairavi
ragas.notes: 20th melakarta; parent of Bhairavi and Sindhu Bhairavi; evokes pathos and devotion; associated with evening hours

ragas.name: Ravichandrika

sources.label: Suja Music — Ravichandrika scale and kritis

ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); vakra raga — ārohana S R2 G3 M1 D2 N2 D2 S', avarohana S' N2 D2 M1 G3 R2 S; rare raga, sung mostly in early concert segments; best known for two Tyagaraja kritis: Makelara and Nirvadhi Sukhada

ragas.name: Surutti
ragas.notes: Janya of Harikambhoji (28th melakarta); bhāshāṅga vakra raga (Subbarama Dikshitar classification); ārohana S R₂ M₁ P N₂ D₂ N₂ Ṡ, avarohana Ṡ N₂ D₂ P M₁ G₃ P M₁ R₂ S; equivalent to Mixolydian mode; evokes devotion and pathos; a favourite raga of the Tyagaraja school

compositions.title: Jagadananda Karaka
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; opens the Pancharatna set; a paean to Rama in the majestic Ghana raga Nata

compositions.title: Dudukugala
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; a self-critical composition in the pathos-laden Ghana raga Gowla

compositions.title: Sadhinchane
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; a devotional composition in the majestic Ghana raga Arabhi

compositions.title: Kana Kana Ruchira
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; set in the auspicious and technically demanding Ghana raga Varali

compositions.title: Entharo Mahanubhavulu
compositions.notes: Pancharatna kriti; considered the crown jewel of the Tyagaraja corpus; a salutation to all great souls; traditionally sung last in the Pancharatna set

compositions.title: maakElaraa vicaaramu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in the rare raga Ravichandrika; one of the two principal compositions that define this raga in the concert repertoire

compositions.title: Gitarthamu
compositions.notes: Tyagaraja kriti in Surutti; a meditation on the meaning of the Gita
